LOG_FAMILY_THEOREM.statement: For integer k >= 2: PCF(a(n) = -k*n^2, b(n) = (k+1)*n + k) = 1/ln(k/(k-1))

real_k_extension.conjecture: Family holds for all real k > 1

LOG_FAMILY_THEOREM.hypergeometric_connection: ln(k/(k-1)) = (1/(k-1)) * _2F1(1,1;2;-1/(k-1)). The PCF is therefore the reciprocal of a scaled _2F1 evaluation via Euler integral.
LOG_FAMILY_THEOREM.proof_method: Closed-form convergents: p_n=(n+1)!k^{n+1}, q_n=(n+1)!*sum k^{n-j}/(j+1). Limit via Taylor series sum x^j/(j+1) = -ln(1-x)/x.

PI_FAMILY_THEOREM.statement: For integer m >= 0: PCF(a(n) = -n(2n-(2m+1)), b(n) = 3n+1) = 2^(2m+1) / (pi * C(2m,m))

even_c_rational.note: Even c values give C(c-1,(c-2)/2) / 2^(c/2-1) type rationals

PI_FAMILY_THEOREM.proof_method: Closed-form convergents: p_n=(2n+1)!!, q_n=(2n+1)!!*sum_{j=0}^n j!/(2j+1)!!. Limit via classical identity pi/2 = sum j!/(2j+1)!!. Full family by binomial recurrence from base case.

CONVERGENCE_COMPARISON.note: All converge at ~0.24 dp/term at depth 500 (precision-limited). Brouncker is faster for low depths.
